casino.description: Tables, wheels, and odds that answer to you. The crown jewel of any family's books.
casino.name: Casino

chop-shop.description: Cars in whole, parts out the back. Your fences pay 65 cents on the dollar for gear instead of 50.
chop-shop.name: Chop Shop

city-hall-annex.description: Permits, zoning, and a clerk who owes you. Turf upkeep runs 25% cheaper citywide.
city-hall-annex.name: City Hall Annex

drug-lab.description: Glassware, burners, and product the street can't get enough of. Runs hot in every sense.
drug-lab.name: Drug Lab

fight-club.description: Two men in, one purse out. The book takes bets on both and the house never bleeds.
fight-club.name: Fight Club

hillcrest-general.description: Private wings and discreet surgeons. You and your crew heal twice as fast.
hillcrest-general.name: Hillcrest General

laundromat.description: Machines hum, books stay clean. A quiet earner that keeps your name off the blotter.
laundromat.name: Laundromat

loan-office.description: Friendly terms, unfriendly collections. The vig compounds either way.
loan-office.name: Loan Office

pawn-shop.description: Everything has a price tag and a story. The estimates are creative; the margins are not.
pawn-shop.name: Pawn Shop

pizzeria.description: Best slice on the block, and nobody asks what moves through the back room.
pizzeria.name: Pizzeria

police-precinct-hq.description: The desk every beat cop answers to. The family holding it pays 30% less for every envelope in the city.
police-precinct-hq.name: Police Precinct HQ

riverside-freight-yard.description: Rails, trucks, and manifests that say whatever you need. Jobs cost 15% less stamina for its holder.
riverside-freight-yard.name: Riverside Freight Yard

safehouse.description: A quiet place with clean sheets and a doctor who makes house calls. Hurt crew get back on their feet twice as fast.
safehouse.name: Safehouse

smuggling-den.description: Crates that skip customs and trucks that drive at night. Volume is the whole business.
smuggling-den.name: Smuggling Den

speakeasy.description: A password, a piano, and shelves of the good stuff. Thirsty city, loyal crowd.
speakeasy.name: Speakeasy

strip-club.description: Velvet, neon, and a cover charge nobody argues with. The till never cools.
strip-club.name: Strip Club

taxi-company.description: Cabs that know every alley and never keep a logbook. Steady fares, better errands.
taxi-company.name: Taxi Company

the-grand-casino.description: The biggest floor in the city, and the house edge answers to whoever holds it.
the-grand-casino.name: The Grand Casino

the-port.description: Cranes, containers, and customs men on the payroll. The store sells to its holder at 10% off.
the-port.name: The Port

union-hall.description: Every crane and picket line in town listens to this floor. Crew wages drop 20% for its holder.
union-hall.name: Union Hall

warehouse.description: Rows of crates nobody inventories. Every till you own holds four more hours of take.
warehouse.name: Warehouse

ammo-bandolier.description: Wears the whole gunfight across your chest.
ammo-bandolier.name: Ammo Bandolier

armored-pinstripe-suit.description: Tailored sharp enough to hide the steel underneath.
armored-pinstripe-suit.name: Armored Pinstripe Suit

ballistic-overcoat.description: A trench coat that takes the bullet and keeps its crease.
ballistic-overcoat.name: Ballistic Overcoat

bar-m1918.description: Walking fire, just like the manual promised.
bar-m1918.name: BAR M1918

baseball-bat.description: Straight off the sandlot, and it never misses a swing.
baseball-bat.name: Baseball Bat

bathtub-gin.description: Juniper and menace, bottled in somebody's basement.
bathtub-gin.name: Bathtub Gin

bennies.description: Truck-stop amphetamines that turn 4 a.m. into noon.
bennies.name: Bennies

black-market-trauma-kit.description: A surgeon's whole bag, minus the surgeon's license.
black-market-trauma-kit.name: Black Market Trauma Kit

blockbuster-charge.description: For when the building itself is the witness.
blockbuster-charge.name: Blockbuster Charge

bolt-cutters.description: Chain-link fences and padlocks give up on sight.
bolt-cutters.name: Bolt Cutters

brass-knuckles.description: Four rings of persuasion for conversations that turn ugly.
brass-knuckles.name: Brass Knuckles

browning-m1919.description: Belt-fed arithmetic that always adds up in your favor.
browning-m1919.name: Browning M1919

burglars-hood.description: Black wool and no face worth remembering.
burglars-hood.name: Burglar's Hood

canvas-tool-belt.description: Every honest tool a dishonest job could need.
canvas-tool-belt.name: Canvas Tool Belt

chemical-smoke-shell.description: Military-grade fog that swallows searchlights whole.
chemical-smoke-shell.name: Chemical Smoke Shell

climbing-rope.description: Forty feet of the quiet way in.
climbing-rope.name: Climbing Rope

coach-gun.description: Once guarded stagecoaches; now it guards your interests.
coach-gun.name: Coach Gun

concussion-flash-charge.description: Rings every bell in a city block's worth of skulls.
concussion-flash-charge.name: Concussion Flash Charge

crepe-sole-creepers.description: Soles so soft the floorboards never get a word in.
crepe-sole-creepers.name: Crepe-Sole Creepers

crowbar.description: The universal key for everything the picks can't charm.
crowbar.name: Crowbar

custom-garand.description: Tuned by a gunsmith who asks no questions.
custom-garand.name: Custom Garand

detective-special.description: The same iron the flatfoots carry, minus the badge.
detective-special.name: Detective Special

disguise-kit.description: A new face for every job and a moustache to spare.
disguise-kit.name: Disguise Kit

dons-armored-topcoat.description: Cashmere over plate; the last word in untouchable.
dons-armored-topcoat.name: Don's Armored Topcoat

door-buster-charge.description: Knocks once, and the door stops being a door.
door-buster-charge.name: Door-Buster Charge

doormans-leather-jacket.description: Thick enough to bounce a bottle and most bad ideas.
doormans-leather-jacket.name: Doorman's Leather Jacket

drum-thompson.description: A hundred rounds in the drum and a headline in every one.
drum-thompson.name: Drum Thompson

enfield-no4.description: British steel with manners and a murderous follow-through.
enfield-no4.name: Enfield No. 4

executioners-balaclava.description: Nothing above the collar but bad news.
executioners-balaclava.name: Executioner's Balaclava

extended-magazine-rig.description: Spare drums and long sticks; the argument never runs dry.
extended-magazine-rig.name: Extended Magazine Rig

field-surgeon-kit.description: Stitches bullet holes in the back of a moving car.
field-surgeon-kit.name: Field Surgeon Kit

fire-axe.description: Borrowed from a firehouse wall and never returned.
fire-axe.name: Fire Axe

first-aid-tin.description: Gauze, iodine, and no questions at the hospital.
first-aid-tin.name: First-Aid Tin

flash-powder-grenade.description: Steals ten seconds of everybody's eyesight.
flash-powder-grenade.name: Flash Powder Grenade

flat-cap.description: Pulls low enough to make eyes forget your face.
flat-cap.name: Flat Cap

french-champagne.description: Celebrating early is a professional hazard worth having.
french-champagne.name: French Champagne

gas-mask.description: Breathes easy while the room learns to regret.
gas-mask.name: Gas Mask

getaway-car-keys.description: A tuned V8 idling exactly where you'll need it.
getaway-car-keys.name: Getaway Car Keys

goofballs.description: Uppers cut with downers — smooth ride, but people notice the eyes.
goofballs.name: Goofballs

grease-gun-m3.description: Cheap, ugly, and it chews through trouble by the crate.
grease-gun-m3.name: M3 Grease Gun

growler-of-beer.description: Warm, flat, and better than nothing after a long night.
growler-of-beer.name: Growler of Beer

hop-tar.description: Old Quarter opium — melts an evening and the trail behind you with it.
hop-tar.name: Hop Tar

hunting-30-06.description: Made for elk; repurposed for bigger game.
hunting-30-06.name: Hunting .30-06

hushed-welrod.description: Coughs once and the room goes quiet forever.
hushed-welrod.name: Hushed Welrod

italian-leather-loafers.description: Imported, immaculate, and worth more than the man staring at them.
italian-leather-loafers.name: Italian Leather Loafers

kingpin-gun-belt.description: Hand-tooled leather for a man who never draws second.
kingpin-gun-belt.name: Kingpin Gun Belt

kingpin-homburg.description: The hat that makes rooms go quiet on arrival.
kingpin-homburg.name: Kingpin Homburg

lead-pipe.description: Plumbing-grade diplomacy, swung from the shadows.
lead-pipe.name: Lead Pipe

leather-holster.description: Keeps the iron close and the draw smooth.
leather-holster.name: Leather Holster

lever-action-carbine.description: Cowboy iron that never went out of style downtown.
lever-action-carbine.name: Lever-Action Carbine

lewis-gun.description: Pan-fed thunder off the back of a truck.
lewis-gun.name: Lewis Gun

lined-greatcoat.description: Wool outside, steel plates where it counts.
lined-greatcoat.name: Lined Greatcoat

lockpick-set.description: Sings through a padlock like it was never there.
lockpick-set.name: Lockpick Set

long-barrel-357.description: Six inches of barrel that make every argument final.
long-barrel-357.name: Long-Barrel .357

m1-garand-surplus.description: Eight rounds and a ping that means somebody's day is over.
m1-garand-surplus.name: M1 Garand Surplus

magnesium-flashbang.description: Daylight in a can, cashed in all at once.
magnesium-flashbang.name: Magnesium Flashbang

military-smoke-grenade.description: A wall of grey you could hide a whole crew behind.
military-smoke-grenade.name: Military Smoke Grenade

molotov-cocktail.description: Bottled trouble with a burning rag for a fuse.
molotov-cocktail.name: Molotov Cocktail

morphine-ampoule.description: Hospital-grade quiet in a glass ampoule — the city stops looking for a man who isn't moving.
morphine-ampoule.name: Morphine Ampoule

nose-candy.description: Uptown powder; makes you brilliant and loud in that order.
nose-candy.name: Nose Candy

padded-work-vest.description: Dockworker's padding that shrugs off knuckles and worse.
padded-work-vest.name: Padded Work Vest

pep-pills.description: Corner-drugstore courage; twenty minutes of feeling bulletproof.
pep-pills.name: Pep Pills

pharma-amps.description: Sealed bottles walked out of a factory loading dock — the pure article.
pharma-amps.name: Pharma-Grade Amps

pineapple-grenade.description: Army surplus that ends meetings early.
pineapple-grenade.name: Pineapple Grenade

pocket-derringer.description: Two shots of last resort tucked in a vest pocket.
pocket-derringer.name: Pocket Derringer

prewar-cognac.description: What the bosses pour when the city belongs to them.
prewar-cognac.name: Prewar Cognac

pump-12-gauge.description: One rack of the slide clears the whole back room.
pump-12-gauge.name: Pump 12-Gauge

radio-scanner.description: Every police call in the city, delivered to your ear.
radio-scanner.name: Radio Scanner

radio-scanner-belt.description: Hears the prowl cars coming three blocks out.
radio-scanner-belt.name: Radio Scanner Belt

riot-gun.description: Built to break up crowds; works fine on partnerships too.
riot-gun.name: Riot Gun

rooftop-special.description: Lives on fire escapes and settles debts from above.
rooftop-special.name: Rooftop Special

rotgut-whiskey.description: Burns going down and takes the day's aches with it.
rotgut-whiskey.name: Rotgut Whiskey

rubber-sole-prowlers.description: Built for rooftops, fire escapes, and clean exits.
rubber-sole-prowlers.name: Rubber-Sole Prowlers

rye-whiskey.description: The honest stuff — puts steel back in the spine.
rye-whiskey.name: Rye Whiskey

safecracker-drill.description: Bores through a vault door while the city sleeps.
safecracker-drill.name: Safecracker Drill

satchel-charge.description: For walls, vaults, and other stubborn opinions.
satchel-charge.name: Satchel Charge

sawed-off-double.description: Cut short so the message travels wide.
sawed-off-double.name: Sawed-Off Double

scoped-springfield.description: Puts the whole city within arm's reach.
scoped-springfield.name: Scoped Springfield

service-45.description: Army surplus with a kick that ends negotiations.
service-45.name: Service .45

shoulder-holster-rig.description: Twin rigs under the jacket, drawn faster than doubt.
shoulder-holster-rig.name: Shoulder Holster Rig

signal-jammer.description: Turns every alarm and radio for a block into dead air.
signal-jammer.name: Signal Jammer

silenced-sten.description: Spits its whole story without raising its voice.
silenced-sten.name: Silenced Sten

single-malt-scotch.description: Older than your rap sheet and twice as smooth.
single-malt-scotch.name: Single Malt Scotch

smoke-canister.description: Turns any room into a getaway.
smoke-canister.name: Smoke Canister

smuggled-bourbon.description: Kentucky's finest, minus the tax stamp.
smuggled-bourbon.name: Smuggled Bourbon

snub-nose-38.description: Short barrel, short temper, fits under any coat.
snub-nose-38.name: Snub-Nose .38

speedball-kit.description: Both directions at once. Works every time until it doesn't.
speedball-kit.name: Speedball Kit

springfield-03.description: Old army bolt gun that still holds a grudge at 300 yards.
springfield-03.name: Springfield '03

steel-toe-boots.description: For doors and shins that won't open politely.
steel-toe-boots.name: Steel-Toe Boots

sten-mk-ii.description: Stamped-metal chatterbox smuggled off a supply truck.
sten-mk-ii.name: Sten Mk II

stiletto-knife.description: A whisper of steel that settles arguments before they start.
stiletto-knife.name: Stiletto Knife

surplus-flak-vest.description: Kept a tail gunner alive; it'll keep you in business.
surplus-flak-vest.name: Surplus Flak Vest

surplus-mp40.description: A war trophy that still speaks fluent trouble.
surplus-mp40.name: Surplus MP40

surplus-steel-helmet.description: Army steel that's already survived worse than you will.
surplus-steel-helmet.name: Surplus Steel Helmet

the-chicago-organ.description: Plays one song, and the whole city dances.
the-chicago-organ.name: The Chicago Organ

the-longshot.description: Ten blocks away is close enough.
the-longshot.name: The Longshot

thompson-m1928.description: The classic Chicago typewriter, ready for dictation.
thompson-m1928.name: Thompson M1928

trench-club.description: Trench salvage with iron studs and old grudges baked in.
trench-club.name: Trench Club

trench-sweeper.description: Cleans a hallway the way the war taught it.
trench-sweeper.name: Trench Sweeper

trophy-mg42.description: Sounds like tearing canvas; leaves nothing to repair.
trophy-mg42.name: Trophy MG42

whisper-scope.description: The last thing they never hear.
whisper-scope.name: Whisper Scope

white-cross.description: Pharmacy-white tablets the long-haul crews swear by.
white-cross.name: White Cross

wingtip-brogues.description: Polished bright enough to distract from the shoulder holster.
wingtip-brogues.name: Wingtip Brogues

wiretap-kit.description: Lets you sit in on conversations you weren't invited to.
wiretap-kit.name: Wiretap Kit

wool-fedora.description: The brim that keeps the streetlights out of your business.
wool-fedora.name: Wool Fedora

worn-oxfords.description: Scuffed enough to blend in anywhere south of Main.
worn-oxfords.name: Worn Oxfords

character.error: Could not reach the family archives. Refresh to try again.
character.impaired: Impaired: −{penalty}% on every job check until you sober up. Doses also restore less stamina while the buzz lasts.

drunk.hint: How drunk you are. −1% on every job check per 10 points, and liquor restores less stamina the drunker you already are. Sobers off 20 per idle hour.
drunk.label: Drunk

health.hint: Physical condition. Failed risky choices can cause damage; armor absorbs part of the hit, while kits and rest restore Health.
health.label: Health

heat.hint: Police attention. Raises every check's difficulty on jobs. Cools 4 per idle hour — or faster via precinct bribes, lay-low work, and doing prison time.
heat.label: Heat

high.hint: How high you are. −1% on every job check per 10 points, and drugs restore less stamina the higher you already are. Sobers off 20 per idle hour.
high.label: High

stamina.hint: Energy for jobs — each contract costs some. Regenerates 25 per hour while you rest; drugs and liquor refill it faster, at a price.
stamina.label: Stamina

charisma.description: Raw personal magnetism. Rolled by charm choices on jobs, and it's what sways a prison guard when you try to bribe your way out of a cell. +1% check chance per level.
charisma.label: Charisma

corruption.description: Knowing who takes envelopes and how thick they need to be. Rolled by deception choices on jobs, and every level shaves the price of paying off the precinct to lower your heat.
corruption.label: Corruption

leadership.description: Running a crew and working a room. Rolled by social choices on jobs — rallying accomplices, leaning on witnesses, commanding respect. +1% check chance per level.
leadership.label: Leadership

muscle.description: Hitting hard and holding your ground. Rolled by force choices on jobs. Equipped power helps every approach, while armor absorbs Health damage after risky failures. +1% check chance per level.
muscle.label: Muscle

stealth.description: Moving unseen and leaving no trace. Rolled by quiet choices on jobs, and it's what carries a prison break attempt. +1% check chance per level.
stealth.label: Stealth

strategy.description: Reading a situation and striking when the gap opens. Rolled by opportunistic choices on jobs. +1% check chance per level.
strategy.label: Strategy

tech.description: Locks, wires, alarms, and explosives — the technical trades. Rolled by technical choices on jobs. +1% check chance per level.
tech.label: Tech

clock.tooltip: City time — one day passes every real hour

header.tagline: Soldiers boost your jobs, staff your rackets, and hold your turf. Pay them on time — the unpaid walk, and the bitter talk.
header.title: Crew

locked.afterRank: (level 10) and the right people start returning your calls.
locked.beforeRank: Nobody works for a nobody. Make
locked.title: Crew

picker.capacity: Bring up to {capacity} — leadership runs the room. ({selected}/{capacity} picked)
picker.nobodyFree: Nobody is free right now. You go alone.

recruitment.empty: The pool is dry. New faces show up tomorrow.
recruitment.heading: Looking for work
recruitment.intro: A fresh pool of faces every day. Signing money up front, wages every game day after.

roster.empty: Nobody on the payroll yet. Look over the candidates below.
roster.heading: Your soldiers

bribed.message: The envelope worked. He walked out with his gear.
bribed.title: Sprung

equipped.message: The gear moved from your stash to his hands.
equipped.title: Geared up

fired.message: He cleared out. The gear on his back went with him.
fired.title: Cut loose

trained.message: Two hours on the mats. He'll come back sharper.
trained.title: In training

unequipped.message: The gear is back in your stash.
unequipped.title: Stored

header.description: Fronts and safe property — nobody can take these from you. The big rackets get built on turf, from the Map.
header.title: Empire

property.empty: You own nothing yet. Every empire starts with one clean storefront.
property.title: Your property

toasts.buySuccess: {name} is yours. Staff it and let it earn.
toasts.collectedRaided: You banked {amount} — but the police raided your {building}. Repair it to reopen.

crew.body: Hire soldiers, arm them, keep them paid.
crew.title: Run your crew

empire.body: Fronts and rackets. Empty the tills before they cap.
empire.title: Mind the books

jobs.body: Contracts on the board. Cash, XP, and trouble.
jobs.title: Work the streets

map.body: Seventy blocks. Paint them your color.
map.title: Take the city

home.intro: Here’s what the city did while you slept.
home.noEditionYet: The first edition of {name} hasn’t hit the stands yet. Make some news.

jobCard.gearConsumable: Consumable — one gets used up per demand on the run.
jobCard.gearIntro: Gear this job may call for — going without makes it harder:
jobCard.gearReusable: Reusable tool — carrying one covers the whole run.

jobs.boardError: The board is quiet. Refresh to try again.

crewPicker.intro: Each soldier boosts checks that lean on his specialty. On a disaster, they share the consequences — and their gear is on the line.
crewPicker.title: Who rides along?

jobs.healError: That kit could not be used. Refresh and try again.
jobs.loadError: Could not reach your contacts. Refresh to try again.

prison.bribeFailure: He takes the money and calls it a donation. You're still here.
prison.bribeSuccess: The guard pockets the envelope and forgets to lock a door. You walk.
prison.escapeFailure: A spotlight, a whistle, and a longer sentence.
prison.escapeSuccess: Over the wall and gone — but the whole city is looking for you now.

jobs.subtitle: Word on the street. Pick a contract and see it through.
jobs.title: Jobs

loadout.loadError: Could not reach the family armory. Refresh to try again.
loadout.title: Loadout

login.subtitle: Identify yourself before entering the operation.

map.conquestClock: THE CONQUEST CLOCK IS RUNNING — the {family} family holds every district. Break their grip before the 72 hours run out.
map.emptyPanel: Pick a block. Gray is unclaimed; colors are families; ★ marks the landmarks money can’t buy.

found.errorMessage: The founding didn't take. Try again.
found.intro: Before you paint the map, the city needs a name to fear and a color to recognize. Choose both carefully — the Ledger will print them.
found.title: Found your family

header.subtitle: Seventy blocks, seven districts, one winner.
header.title: The City

locked.afterRank: (level 40). Keep working — the city will still be here.
locked.beforeRank: The map opens when you make
locked.title: The City

picker.attackIntro: Committed soldiers add their power to the assault. A rout costs bodies and gear.
picker.claimIntro: The takeover plays like a job — your crew's specialties boost its checks.
picker.defenseIntro: Posted defenders add double their power to this block's defense.

toasts.attackPartial: {turf} held, but you dented its defenses. Hit it again.
toasts.attackRout: A rout at {turf}. Your soldiers paid for it.
toasts.buildMessage: The racket opens damaged-free and starts earning.

turf.takeoverHint: A takeover plays out like a job — win it and the flag goes up.

acceptedEquipment.gearReusable: Reusable exact match · no consumed-item power
acceptedEquipment.gearSingleUse: Single-use · one consumed on a matching choice
acceptedEquipment.gearSingleUsePower: Single-use · Power {power} on a matching choice
acceptedEquipment.lockedPower: Locked for this job: {character} character + {equipment} equipment power.
acceptedEquipment.title: Accepted equipment
acceptedEquipment.unavailable: Accepted equipment details are unavailable for this older job.

equipment.locked: Locked: this move needs a {gear} and you don't have one
equipment.none: No equipment required — odds shown as-is
equipment.readyConsumable: Ready: {item} — single-use; one is consumed if chosen, and readiness is included in the odds
equipment.readyReusable: Ready: {item} — reusable; readiness is included in the odds

choice.healthRisk: Health risk: failure can cause damage; accepted armor is already included in protection.
choice.upfrontCost: Upfront cost: {amount} is paid the moment you commit — win or lose.

healthPanel.healingWindow: Healing opens after your first choice and closes when the job ends.
healthPanel.noKits: No healing kits in your stash.
healthPanel.title: Live Health

momentum.bandsSummary: Finish at {successAt}+ for a clean success · above {jackpotAbove} is jackpot · below {failureBelow} ends in arrest
momentum.title: Momentum

runner.fixerWorking: The fixer is working out the details…

skillXp.none: No skill experience gained on this job.
skillXp.title: Skill experience earned

newspaper.bounty: (Take a block from the {family} family and the {amount} is yours.)
newspaper.empty: No edition has hit the stands yet. The presses run once a day — give the city time to make some news.
newspaper.reign: Under the reign of the {family} family
newspaper.tagline: All the crime that’s fit to print

notifications.empty: The street is quiet. Nothing new.

playerNameDialog.subtitle: This is how the city will know you. Choose carefully — it cannot be changed later.
playerNameDialog.title: Choose your name

bribe.body: {cost} changes hands whether he bites or not. Your charisma does the talking — {chance}% odds.
bribe.title: Bribe a guard

prison.cooldown: The guards are watching you too closely. Next attempt in {time}.

escape.body: Stealth carries you over the wall — {chance}% odds. Fail and the sentence grows; succeed and the manhunt adds heat.
escape.title: Break out

prison.flavor: The cell is cold, the coffee is worse, and the city is getting along fine without you.
prison.serveNote: — serving the full stretch also cools your heat
prison.title: You’re doing time

rankings.countdown: CONQUEST COUNTDOWN — the {family} family holds all seven districts. About {hours} hours remain to break their grip.
rankings.description: Respect accrues from held turf (weighted by district), battles won, landmarks controlled, and jackpot jobs. Conquer every district stronghold and hold it {hours} hours to take the city outright — otherwise the most respected family wins when the season ends on {date}.

table.caption: Season standings
table.description: Every family with a name in the city, most respected first.
table.empty: Nobody has planted a flag yet. The city waits.

rankings.title: Rankings

signOutModal.intro: You are about to sign out. The family will keep your affairs in order until you return.
signOutModal.signOutDescription: End the session and head back to the door.
signOutModal.stayDescription: Return to the operation as if nothing happened.
signOutModal.title: Sure you want out?

store.catalogError: The store is shuttered. Refresh to try again.

header.tagline: Cash buys the gear; jobs decide if you needed it. Locked pieces open up as you level.
header.title: The Store

store.noMatches: No gear matches your current search and filters. Try another name, category, or required level.

toast.buyErrorMessage: The fence isn't answering. Try again.

street.impairedTip: Being high or drunk lowers the pass chance of every check on jobs you take, and doses restore less stamina. It wears off while you rest.
